title_en: A Month of Going All Out
excerpt_en: 2024/03/3 Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "A Month of Going All Out." ▼Completely Mastered Masu Photo Shoots Overseas So, today marks exactly one month since I came to Cebu.
body_en:
2024/03/3

Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about the theme "A Month of Going All Out."

▼Completely Mastered Masu Photo Shoots Overseas

So, today marks exactly one month since I came to Cebu.

It's the last day of week four.

And today, I had Masu Photo shoots from the afternoon.

I had originally scheduled

5 shoots,

but one person said,

"My sister caught a cold, so I can't make it."

And another said,

"I have a hangover, so I can't come."

Both of these were confirmed on Saturday (the day before),

yet they canceled on the day of the shoot.

#I'm used to it by now

I've learned that if you schedule a few extra shoots in Cebu,

some people will cancel,

so it actually works out just right lol

The recruitment for this round of Masu Photo shoots was

also entirely through Instagram story ads.

This month, I was able to gather data showing that

the "Masu Photo" project

does resonate with some people.

And combined with the data from the shoots I did in Kuala Lumpur last year, it seems that for Southeast Asian regions,

Instagram-based Masu Photo shoots are effective.

Whether this

will work in America or Europe,

I still don't know.

But getting back to the topic,

today's shoots ended up being 3 in total.

Once again, as my assistant,

I brought along a 20-year-old college student I'd become friends with here.

He said he wanted to see my work in action.

The models for the shoots

pretty much all show up 10 to 40 minutes late as a given.

#I'm used to this too

The first person was a student.

She loves Japanese culture and content,

which is why she applied this time.

She said it was her first time seeing a masu.

Since it was hot, we wrapped up the shoot in about 30 minutes,

and spent the rest of the time chatting.

We talked about all sorts of things,

and we're going to grab dinner on the 5th (the day after tomorrow)!

It's wonderful to see

connections being formed like this.

The second person was a working professional.

She also does cosplay,

and was super enthusiastic about the Masu Photo shoot.

She was used to being photographed,

so it went really smoothly!!

Up to this point, everything was going amazingly well!

And then, the third and final person.

This one was a bit of a struggle.

Someone who wasn't great at communicating showed up,

and she wasn't active as a model either.

*My screening process was too lenient

30 minutes past the meeting time,

someone came to our meeting spot,

and they clearly didn't look like they were dressed for a shoot.

I thought a stranger was approaching us

just to ask for directions,

but she kept hanging around nearby,

so I asked, "Could you be (showing my Instagram account) this person?"

And she said, "Yes, that's me."

No makeup, no effort to dress up.

I was honestly surprised.

This is entirely my own shortcoming,

but I wasn't able to

bring out her best qualities to the fullest.

*I can't exactly carry a makeup kit with me

In the end, instead of being the model herself,

she ended up taking photos of the FOMUS masu

with her own smartphone and having fun with that.

It was supposed to be a modeling gig,

but instead of modeling, she photographed the masu.

There are really all kinds of people out there, and it's fascinating!!

You don't get experiences like this very often lol

And after all my shoot work was done,

I did a Masu Photo shoot with three people I'd become friends with at the language school:

a Korean guy the same age as me, a Chinese girl, and a Japanese mom!

It was just one shot,

but they had a blast!

It really does make me happy when people

enjoy the Masu Photo experience!

▼Off to the Night Market

With the language school crew,

we headed straight to a night market near the shoot location.

We each bought food from the stalls,

and had dinner together.

After that, we grabbed a drink at a nearby bar,

and had a good old girls' chat session for the first time in a while.

Since coming to language school,

I feel like my heart has gotten younger. lol

On my days off,

I'm usually doing shoots or going to drinking parties,

so I've been increasing my opportunities to use English.

I really felt throughout this month that

how you spend your days off is incredibly important.

▼A Refreshing Makeover Consultation

Actually, before the shoots, my assistant and I

went to a hair salon in the mall where the shoots were held.

This student is 20 years old,

and he hadn't yet built confidence in his appearance.

Still, he'd gathered the courage to come study abroad in Cebu,

and personally, I felt like "I see my past self in him, and if I can help him break out of his shell even a little."

I can't change who someone is on the inside,

but I can support them in changing their appearance.

About 5 years ago, while working as a hotel staff member,

I was also a photographer for dating apps,

and I did grooming consultations on the side.

So with that background,

first I took him to the hair salon

to get his hair and eyebrows tidied up.

I showed the stylist reference images of the hairstyle I had in mind, and after the cut, I asked for a bit more trimming here and there.

On top of that, at UNIQLO,

I picked out a fresh, clean look — the classic college student style.

For about 5,000 yen, we changed his hair, eyebrows, and clothes.

I myself was

a plain guy through high school,

so I totally get how he feels.

If this experience leads to an inner change for him,

that would personally make me really happy.

So all in all,

today from the afternoon,

I accompanied him for a haircut, picked out clothes,

did three shoots,

and then had dinner and drinks at a bar with friends.

It was a great day off? indeed!

title_en: Month Two of Cebu Life Begins
excerpt_en: 2024/03/4 Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Month Two of Cebu Life Begins." ▼Going All Out on Both Work and English This article is being written on 2024/03/04. In other words,
body_en:
2024/03/4

Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about the theme "Month Two of Cebu Life Begins."

▼Going All Out on Both Work and English

This article is being written on 2024/03/04.

That means month two has officially begun.

Looking back on this first month,

it really flew by,

and all I can think is how fast time passes.

If things go according to plan,

I'll be heading to Ireland from July 2024,

where I plan to expand the masu brand "FOMUS" into Europe.

To prepare for that, I'm also planning to

do about a month of language study in Ireland,

but honestly, I'm starting to feel like one month won't be nearly enough.

*I've already been approved for an Irish working holiday visa.

The reason is that

improving my English is a given, but

building strong connections with fellow language students is simply too short a time.

I've actually seen the people who came to Cebu

for just one month,

and it ends in a flash,

so for some, the relationships they build are limited.

In that case,

I feel like it's better to stay as long as possible.

A full two months would be tough, but about a month and a half might work.

(That's how I feel right now)

I got sidetracked right away, but

the language school in Cebu is going well.

Mornings start with a run, and until evening it's an immersive English life with one-on-one and group classes.

At night, it's FOMUS activities and English prep and review.

Compared to when I was in Japan, the time I can dedicate to business has drastically decreased, and I need to work more efficiently — and personally, I feel like my work efficiency has actually improved as a result.

As I wrote in yesterday's article,

starting this week, I've stopped doing Masu Photo shoots with the general public, and I'm preparing to make offers to people who are actively working as professional models.

I've been talking to the school teachers about these preparations,

and some of them find it really interesting.

One teacher even offered to introduce me to a model friend of hers!

Nothing's confirmed yet, but

I'm one step closer to doing a

"photographer x Japanese traditional craft" shoot

with a Filipino model (professional).

If I can do a Masu Photo shoot with a pro model in Cebu,

it'll make it easier to work with pro models in other countries too.

In these remaining 2 months, I really want to secure a shoot with a local pro model.

Regarding the shoots, I'm also doing research in parallel

on finding models and scouting locations,

and things are starting to come together.

How many models out there will find our project interesting??

And what kind of results will this approach bring?

To be honest, this is my first time trying this,

so I have no idea how it'll turn out right now.

Still, nothing moves forward unless I make the offer, so I'm preparing thoroughly and pushing ahead.

And while still in Cebu,

I've also built a system where I produce short movies for FOMUS character "KUKU" promotions once a week and distribute them across various social media platforms.

Video production is handled by a professional,

so my role is to create the background illustrations and post them.

It takes about an hour to complete, so the workload is light.

I have various other projects on my plate,

but I'm steadily working through them in order of priority.

That said, improving my English

is the most important thing!!

▼A Four-Person Room That's Totally International

My room is a four-person room, and

right now it's me, a Chinese person, a Korean person, and a Vietnamese person —

a truly multinational room.

There are opportunities to talk in the room,

and with all sorts of languages flying around,

it's quite interesting.

*Not just English

My roommates are all interesting people,

and I'm able to live without feeling too self-conscious.

*The only unavoidable issue is that the shower, toilet, and sink are all in one space

Beyond that, even with people in different rooms, I've been getting more chances to talk with people of various nationalities, and the environment is gradually shaping up.

That said, the ratio of time I spend on work

instead of self-studying English is still too high,

and I always have this feeling that I want to manage my time better and dedicate more to studying English.

This is something

I need to improve, and it's this week's challenge.

And so,

Cebu life has entered its second month.

I'm going to build solid English skills!!

title_en: Making Cebu Life Even More Exciting
excerpt_en: 2024/03/5 Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Making Cebu Life Even More Exciting." ▼Model Hunting & Venue Scouting Lately, I've been searching for pro models in Cebu while also looking for shoot venues.
body_en:
2024/03/5

Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about the theme "Making Cebu Life Even More Exciting."

▼Model Hunting & Venue Scouting

Lately,

I've been searching for pro models in Cebu

while also scouting for venues.

My experience from past photography work is really coming in handy here.

This might not be widely known, but

Cebu Island actually doesn't have beautiful beaches.

The neighboring "Mactan Island," where the airport is,

has several beaches.

There are public beaches, of course,

as well as multiple private beaches belonging to luxury resort hotels.

I looked for beaches

that could work for Masu Photo shoots,

but all the beautiful ones are private beaches at resort hotels.

Of course,

the entrance fees are tens of times higher compared to public beaches.

However,

there's a reason I want to do this even if it means paying that much.

For the shoots in Cebu this time,

I'm going to challenge something I couldn't do before.

That is, paying a fee

to hire pro models for Masu Photo shoots.

As for why I couldn't do this before —

I simply had zero desire to pay Japanese models to take "Masu Photos."

I wanted people who genuinely found the FOMUS brand vision and my activities interesting to participate.

These two were the big reasons.

In Japan, even if people haven't used a "masu" themselves,

plenty of people know what it is,

so they can picture holding a masu for a photo.

However, overseas, people don't know about FOMUS, let alone what a "masu" even is.

In other words, if I can approach this well,

masu = FOMUS could become a thing,

and furthermore, it increases the possibility of establishing the masu as a communication tool that connects people around the world.

So,

I'm planning to pay a fee and conduct shoots

with people who find FOMUS's activities interesting,

want to participate in Masu Photo,

and work professionally as models.

There are 2 months left, but

if I can pull off Masu Photo shoots with professional models, it'll make it much easier for pro models in other countries to participate going forward, and it'll make FOMUS's outreach dramatically easier.

So,

I'm thinking of having them participate by covering the private beach fee and paying a modeling fee.

As I wrote in the previous article,

by coincidence, a friend of one of my English teachers is a model, and that model said she'd bring along other model friends. Nothing's confirmed yet, but

I might be able to shoot with several models.

I've also made a pamphlet,

so preparations are all set.

Now all that's left is

having the teacher reach out to her model friend

with the Masu Photo shoot request.

The fees to be paid

will likely vary depending on the model's level,

and I need to get used to navigating these situations

to toughen myself up,

so I plan to tackle this head-on.

By the way,

I found an amazing beach location!

I'll report back when I get to do the shoot.

https://masuphoto.theshop.jp/secret_ec/secret_ec_auths/login

▼(Personally) Assembling the Best Teachers

As for life at the language school, we're now in week 5.

I've been swapping teachers quite a bit,

and I personally assembled what I consider the ultimate teacher team.

But one of them left the school, and I was forced to switch to a different teacher.

*If I had known in advance, I would have made the switch myself

*So I ended up having a teacher changed on me without my say

So,

starting Monday I had a completely unknown teacher,

and partly due to my own lack of ability,

I just couldn't catch what she was saying.

*Hard to hear

In a work setting, you can't swap out a partner just because their English is hard to understand, but this is a language school.

There are plenty of teachers,

and you can make changes.

If you submit the paperwork at the office by Wednesday (3 days after the week starts),

you can change, so

I went to submit my request today (Tuesday).

That said, many teacher slots are already filled,

so there aren't a lot of teachers to choose from.

Plus, the teachers I know are very few.

But there was one teacher I'd had my eye on.

I may have mentioned this in an article a few days ago, but

last Friday at the bar with some teachers, there was a female teacher I was personally into.

I'd exchanged Instagram with her,

and I'd also gotten her name,

so I wanted to take her class.

But she was a teacher for the CA training course,

and apparently people in regular courses couldn't take her class.

However, I really wanted to learn from her! (partly just wanting to see her), so I talked to the HR person and

they said I could make the switch!

It pays to ask!

However,

the time slot I wanted to change (7th period) was already

taken by another student's class with that teacher.

Meaning, I couldn't change 7th period...

What to do???

So I compared the class schedule listing teachers' names

with the student roster,

and found that one of my current teachers

had 7th period free.

I was originally taking that teacher's class

in 1st period.

So,

for 7th period:

- Teacher I wanted to change → OUT

- My 1st period teacher → IN

And in the now-open 1st period slot,

I placed the teacher I wanted.

This change takes effect next Monday,

so it's not confirmed yet at this point, but I did everything I could this week.

If it doesn't work out, there's nothing more I can do.

This is all

to improve my English communication skills!!

I go all out in everything I do.

*I have zero hesitation about changing teachers

*I don't dislike the teachers — I'm just prioritizing English improvement

▼Dinner Out Tonight

With all that going on, I thought I'd written quite a lot for this article, but

there was one more big thing that happened today.

I had dinner plans with the college student model I shot with last week,

so today I headed to IT Park in Cebu

along with the guy who assisted me.

The model also

brought two of her friends along.

Five of us for dinner.

What surprised me was how young everyone was!

Of course they're college students, so they're young, but

they were 18, 19, and 20.

I was the only one in my late twenties. lol

I never imagined I'd be going out to eat with people 10 years younger than me, people I didn't even meet through language school.

But being able to communicate directly in English with locals in this kind of setting is personally really exciting for me.

On top of that, I gave them a rough overview of FOMUS,

and they found it interesting.

The fact that girls in their early twenties from overseas showed interest in masu is precisely because of content and services like Masu Photo.

Being able to confirm things like this one by one

is one of the reasons I'm glad I came here.

Well, this ended up being

quite a hefty article for the day.

The fact that I had this much to output

means I had that much input.

Even after a month, there are always new discoveries and lessons,

and I'm looking forward to my continued activities in Cebu.

Making sure to get Masu Photos done too!

*Currently recruiting models both domestically and internationally

excerpt_en: 2024/03/6 Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Just Gotta Do It!" ▼Making a Pamphlet Today I'll be talking about FOMUS again. So, as I've written many times in these articles,
body_en:
2024/03/6

Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about the theme "Just Gotta Do It!"

▼Making a Pamphlet

Today I'll be talking about FOMUS again.

So, as I've written many times in these articles,

I've wrapped up the Masu Photo shoots aimed at the general public and am now working on getting shoots set up with people who work in entertainment or as models.

If some random Japanese guy who showed up just a month ago suddenly tells a model,

"I want you to hold a masu and let me take your photo!"

they're not even going to give me a second glance.

So I've been preparing various things for about two weeks now, and as the dates, venue, and target people gradually came together, I put together a pamphlet.

Here it is.

.jpeg)

*This is just a portion of the pamphlet

Then, I sent offer messages via Instagram

to about 100 Filipino models

who didn't have their email addresses listed.

(*I'll send emails to those with listed addresses tomorrow)

But here's where I made a mistake!!

Due to Instagram's settings for models,

you can only send one message

if you're not following each other!

In other words, I couldn't send the pamphlet I made

to anyone except those who had their DMs open!

I had absolutely no idea about this.

So I poured my heart and soul into a single message

and sent it to each person one by one.

Not being able to send the pamphlet means

I can't convey the finer details or the visual concept, which is a tough problem.

But there's nothing I can do about it,

so all I can do is wait for replies.

Fortunately, for models who have their email addresses listed,

I can send them the pamphlet along with a message,

so if they at least see it, I might be able to improve my chances a little!

I've realized that for an ordinary Japanese person to set up appointments with models overseas and take photos, connections and networks are really essential.

Even in Japan, a project about taking photos while holding a masu is pretty niche, so doing it overseas means I know there'll be tons of walls in my way. lol

Man, nothing comes easy!

On top of all of this, there's FOMUS work and my own activities to handle,

plus English studies on top of that,

so for these 3 months,

the only real rest I get is when I'm sleeping!

Every day truly flies by.

So anyway,

today's article ended up a bit shorter,

but consider this a status update.

So, what developments lie ahead??

I'll keep you posted when there's progress!

title_en: The Importance of Changing Your Environment
excerpt_en: 2024/03/7 Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "The Importance of Changing Your Environment." ▼Environment Matters It's the middle of week 5 since coming to Cebu. For the class style, rather than leaving it to the teacher, I decide
body_en:
2024/03/7

Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about the theme "The Importance of Changing Your Environment."

▼Environment Matters

It's the middle of week 5 since coming to Cebu.

For the class style,

rather than leaving it up to the teacher, I've been deciding things myself to a degree.

Instead of using textbooks,

I'm studying business-related vocabulary and idioms that I want to learn.

And instead of Japanese-accented English,

I'm doing training for fluent pronunciation and such,

cramming everything in at a rapid pace.

Thanks to these efforts, compared to a month ago,

I've gotten reasonably better at speaking.

My interactions with teachers have also increased,

and many of them now know that I run a "masu" brand and work as a photographer.

Also,

my room has 4 roommates, each from a different country,

and our exchanges in English are growing more and more.

Beyond that,

after classes, I go up to the rooftop

to send English offers to local models,

and chat with others who come up to the rooftop too.

It's a great environment for learning English.

Over these 3 months,

I've invested about 1.3 million yen in myself,

and I'll make sure to build the skills

to earn that back,

while also cherishing

the priceless human connections.

Living away from Japan like this

is not difficult for me at all.

After all,

I spent about 2 years as an address hopper in Japan,

traveling all over the country and abroad,

so my body and mind were already trained

to survive in any environment.

On top of that,

as an address hopper, you're constantly on the move, but

for the first time in about 2 and a half years,

I'm staying in one place,

and it feels kind of strange.

I used to travel around with my suitcase every time,

but now I just go back and forth between the classroom and the dorm.

That's precisely why I tend to not get enough exercise,

so I've been using the treadmill

to run about 3 to 5 km every day.

Part of the reason is that I really want to participate in the Medoc Marathon held in Bordeaux, France, in September.

*I missed the first round of

registration for the Medoc Marathon without realizing it.

There's a second round of applications next week, apparently.

In this way,

since coming to Cebu,

my lifestyle, relationships, and cross-cultural interactions

have all changed dramatically.

Personally, I think this is

extremely important,

because up until a few years ago, I was living in my hometown of Saitama.

In 2019, I moved to Akasaka and got to know the Minato Ward area.

In 2021, I gave up my apartment in Akasaka

and became a homeless address hopper,

traveling all across Japan.

Since graduating from university,

I've been periodically changing my environment.

Honestly, I used to think being an address hopper was the ultimate way to change your environment, but that was only within Japan.

In 2024, I've decided to expand FOMUS while moving around overseas,

February to end of April: Philippines

May to June: Japan and somewhere else

From July: Ireland

It's a life of constantly changing environments.

The information I'm exposed to is always different,

and personally it makes for incredibly interesting and stimulating days.

To change yourself,

relying on willpower alone tends to fizzle out after three days.

For people,

when the environment changes, thinking changes.

When thinking changes, you take action.

When you take action, some kind of result comes out.

From that result, you move to a new environment,

and when the environment changes, thinking changes again...

It's this loop.

In other words, by forcibly changing your environment,

your thinking changes, it shows in your actions,

and when results come from that,

you reassess your environment.

I'm living proof of that myself.

I can't yet say I've seen much of the world, but

it certainly doesn't hurt to keep this sense in mind.

title_en: A Day Savoring the Best of Language Study Abroad
excerpt_en: 2024/03/8 Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "A Day Savoring the Best of Language Study Abroad." ▼Studying Abroad Is Fun So, today is Friday. The college students who started at the same time as me and became my friends are graduating here.
body_en:
2024/03/8

Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about the theme "A Day Savoring the Best of Language Study Abroad."

▼Studying Abroad Is Fun

So, today is Friday.

The college students who started studying abroad at the same time as me

and became my friends are graduating here.

With this, almost everyone who started on February 4th

has graduated.

Language study abroad during college spring break

is inevitably short.

If they took a leave of absence

and used language study abroad combined with a working holiday, it would be different,

but short-term study abroad really does feel short.

That said, I'm only here for 3 months too,

so it's not that long either.

How to increase opportunities to use English?

That's become incredibly important.

The quickest way to increase these opportunities is

to interact with people who are also

here for language study.

My school has a lot of people

in their 20s and 30s, which makes it easy to connect.

So today, we went out for Korean food with an internationally diverse group including the Korean and Chinese students who graduated today.

It turned into a fun gathering with Chinese, Korean, and English all flying around.

Since coming to Cebu,

I've been consciously trying to use English,

but when people start talking in Chinese and Korean,

I obviously can't understand lol

But they explain in English afterwards,

so I can understand.

I think this is really interesting, because

even when Chinese or Korean is spoken,

since the others can't speak Japanese,

the translation ends up being in English.

I, as a Japanese person, grasp the content

by listening to that English, and

somehow, I'm actually understanding it lol

In other words, even when the translation is in English rather than Japanese, I can follow the conversation.

Before coming to Cebu,

I would have given up listening to English pretty quickly,

but now it's different.

Environment really is important, and

because I have such a strong desire to communicate,

I'm starting to really think before I speak.

Language study abroad is about learning English, of course, but

it's also a place where you meet and interact with people from all sorts of countries.

There's no age restriction for enrollment,

so whether you're a college student, a working professional,

or retired — if you have the desire, you can do it.

I'm fully savoring the best parts

of studying abroad right now.

And this might be coincidence, but

there are surprisingly many business owners here.

And they're in their 20s!

A Korean girl my age used to own a cafe business but sold it off,

and one of my roommates

is a chef, and another is a supermarket owner.

Everyone has their own thing going on.

I'm a photographer myself,

and I run a brand for the traditional Japanese craft "masu,"

but having grown up in completely different environments

and meeting like this is really fun

and incredibly stimulating.

*Both in terms of English and business

These kinds of opportunities

are possible precisely because I came for language study,

and the self-investment has been worth it.

That's exactly why I'm studying abroad in my late twenties,

but I don't regret not studying English more

earlier in my twenties.

Rather, I'm glad I realized it at this timing,

and there are people I could only have met and befriended because of this timing.

In the end, these were encounters that were meant to be.

In this world where the more you move,

the more results come your way,

the most important thing is to just try!

I truly believe that.

Week 5 of my Cebu activities is about to wrap up, and

once again, I'm glad I came.

Being able to stay longer and study English intensively is huge for me.

So with that,

tomorrow starts a 2-day break.

I won't be doing Masu Photo activities — right now I'm just sending emails and waiting for replies.

Tomorrow, a friend invited me to go to the ocean,

so I'm going to go check things out!

excerpt_en: 2024/03/9 Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Island Hopping with Friends." ▼First Real Sightseeing at the End of Week 5 It's Saturday, so the school is closed. And since we're currently in the offer phase for Masu Photo shoots, there are no shoots scheduled.
body_en:
2024/03/9

Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about the theme "Island Hopping with Friends."

▼First Real Sightseeing at the End of Week 5

It's Saturday, so the school is closed.

And since we're currently in the offer phase for Masu Photo shoots, there are no shoots scheduled.

So,

this was my first time doing proper sightseeing! lol

In February, I was scheduling shoots with locals on every school holiday, so it was mostly shopping malls rather than tourist spots — meaning I hadn't really done any sightseeing.

But the friends around me,

of course, don't have work to bring along,

so their days off are all about vacation!

I'd been hearing their stories too,

so I'd been thinking about making time in March

to go somewhere.

That's when my Chinese roommate invited me to go to the beach, so I decided to join.

I also invited a Japanese person and a Chinese person I'd gotten close with at last night's drinking party, making it a group of 4.

We headed to Banban Beach at the tip of Mactan Island, where the airport is, rather than Cebu Island itself.

Since I was the one invited this time,

I hadn't done any research at all, but

this beach wasn't that big, so even though we came in the morning, there wasn't much to do!

The water was shallow and murky, and it was crowded too...

While I was thinking about what to do,

we ran into one of those tourist touts you find everywhere.

He said for 1,500 pesos, they'd take us by boat to another island for snorkeling.

Since there wasn't much to do at this beach,

I negotiated and managed to get it down to 1,200 pesos.

*I really wanted to get it down to 1,000 pesos though

At this beach,

the boat owners and the touts work as a team.

The touts catch tourists

and bring them to the boats.

The touts probably get a percentage of the revenue.

At other beaches, sometimes the boat owners approach tourists directly, but

here the system was well established.

So we rode the boat for about 30 minutes to a small island.

The island they took us to wasn't that big, but it was a peaceful place.

We hadn't eaten anything since morning, so we decided to eat on this island.

That said, the only food options were seafood.

We bought huge lobsters, shrimp, clams, crab, and more and had them boiled.

Since there were 4 of us, we bought in bulk, but it was a bit too much.

Honestly, the lobster and shrimp were thoroughly cooked so that was fine, but the shellfish were really scary! The outside of the shells was covered in barnacles! lol

But since we'd ordered them, I ended up eating quite a bit.

*I'm writing this article the same evening, and so far no issues

Hope I don't get an upset stomach...

With our bellies full, we headed to the next destination.

After about 15 minutes of rocking on the boat,

we arrived at the middle of nowhere in the ocean.

But

there were already lots of boats there.

Yep, this was the snorkeling spot.

Our tour included snorkeling equipment, so we got in the water.

As you'd expect from the Philippines,

we could clearly see all sorts of fish.

Personally, I think snorkeling takes a lot of energy, so an aquarium is good enough for me.

Then we headed to the next spot,

which was another snorkeling spot.

Everyone else went snorkeling, but I was feeling incredibly tired, and my body was telling me it would be dangerous to get in, so I stayed on the boat and lay down for a nap.

Sleeping on a boat is actually amazing.

*It reminded me of the couple I met 3 years ago in Miyako Island who lived on a boat.

Then we took the boat back to Mactan Island.

After that, we took a Grab back to school,

but probably due to traffic, the fare was 3 times more than on the way there.

That said, since there were 4 of us,

it came out to only about 1,000 yen per person.

The seafood lunch was a bit pricey,

but all in all, the day cost about 6,000 yen.

It was a really meaningful day.

▼You Can Learn a Lot by Having Fun

What I felt again today is that it's important to actually go out and have fun.

Opportunities to hang out in the Philippines with two Chinese people you met at school don't come around often.

It deepens our bonds.

Also, there were plenty of locals

trying to get money from us for one reason or another.

Things like "This is a private beach,

so you need to pay 500 yen per person" were common.

They probably target us because

well-mannered Japanese people tend to pay up.

I ignored them, of course.

Also, it had been a while since I'd adjusted to others rather than just doing my own thing.

Group dynamics, you know.

Especially with different cultures,

the feeling of everyone thinking and moving together was something I hadn't felt in a while.

*I felt this precisely because I'd been acting freely on my own until now

There are limits to what you can do alone, so having this kind of opportunity was really great.

So,

this article ended up being just a diary entry.

It's true I was out having fun,

but during the boat rides, I was doing FOMUS-related work with people in Cebu.

And so, here's today's

newly updated KUKU video!

https://vt.tiktok.com/ZSFfesja4/

excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Building the Ultimate Environment." ▼Everything but Sleep Is English and Business Week 6 of being in Cebu has begun. Yesterday was a full day of sightseeing, but today I stayed in,
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about the theme "Building the Ultimate Environment."

▼Everything but Sleep Is English and Business

Week 6 of being in Cebu has begun.

Yesterday was a full day of sightseeing, but

today I didn't go out at all.

I spent my time on the school rooftop and in the study room,

doing FOMUS work

and studying English.

As for FOMUS work,

it's been about finding Filipino models

and sending them offer emails.

I've sent emails to about 100 people so far,

but since it's the weekend, I've only gotten 4 replies.

However,

due to budget and scheduling issues,

I haven't reached the phase where any shoots are confirmed yet.

Beyond that,

there's preparation for FOMUS's future

overseas expansion (Europe),

and preparations for

a FOMUS-hosted digital art exhibition right after I return to Japan.

Furthermore,

regarding how to advance FOMUS in Japan going forward,

the current trends have shifted from the original plan,

so I need to rethink things.

I've been thinking about all this constantly, so it doesn't really feel like work.

That's where things stand with FOMUS.

Also, something interesting happened.

I'm not sure how it'll actually pan out, but

one of the models I shot Masu Photos with in Cebu

reached out to me directly

and invited me to her upcoming birthday party.

That said, it's as a photographer hire.

"The photos from last time were so good that you were the first photographer who came to mind."

What a wonderful offer

for me as a photographer.

So,

the party is apparently next week,

and I plan to go after school.

*I might even get to use FOMUS masu there

And now.

Switching to the English side of things.

I've completed 5 weeks of classes in Cebu.

Honestly, since I've been focusing on speaking,

my speaking has leveled up a bit,

but my listening is still weak.

I feel like listening skills only improve through lots of conversation, so instead of the school-provided textbooks, I only use those with teachers who are good at teaching fundamentals.

With teachers I actually want to talk to, I've arranged conversation-focused classes (I tell the teachers directly).

The best way is to have conversations with people you want to talk to

And as I wrote a little while back,

about the teacher swap.

I just checked, and

the schedule change went through properly.

When it comes to business, I don't need motivation —

I naturally go full throttle all the time.

But for English, while I do have a strong desire to be able to listen and speak,

since I'm here anyway, I want to talk with someone I genuinely think "I really want to talk to this person!"

So,

at a drinking session with teachers 2 weeks ago, I found

a really beautiful teacher, and

I negotiated with the HR person

to get her assigned as my teacher!

She's apparently a teacher for the CA (cabin attendant) candidate program, but since there aren't many students in the CA course right now, I was able to pick her.

*I partly chose this school because I wanted to befriend CA candidates, and it paid off in this unexpected way lol

I now have her for my 1st period class (starting at 8 AM), so I'm even looking forward to waking up in the morning!! lol

*Half joking

From that teacher,

I plan to practice everyday and business conversation,

and have her point out things she notices about my speech and grammar mistakes, for more practical training.

By the way, my 2nd period teacher is also

one I "nominated" 2 weeks ago,

because I had the same feeling of "I want to talk to this person!"

*She's cute

With this teacher too, I skip textbooks and

dedicate the time to pronunciation practice (since I have Japanese-accented English that I want to fix).

In any case, I'll need to use English for business

in just a few months,

so I need to improve my English at a rapid pace.

*That's the whole reason for studying in Cebu

▼Added More English Classes

Furthermore, starting week 6,

I enrolled in a "Special Class."

This class is designed

to build English vocabulary, and consists of:

Every week Mon-Fri, 7:00 AM — 15-minute test

Every week Mon-Fri, 7:00 PM — 45-minute class

as a set.

This class is free to take, but

if you miss 4 times, you get a warning.

Because FOMUS activities had been hectic,

I hadn't been taking it,

but now that one of FOMUS's big projects

is wrapped up, I'm starting this week.

This means

my morning runs, which I pair with a shower,

become harder to fit in.

So after some trial and error,

starting tomorrow, my schedule will be:

6:00 Wake up

6:30 Get ready

7:00 Special Class

7:20 Breakfast

8:00 Classes start

4:30 PM Running

5:30 PM Shower

6:00 PM Dinner

7:00 PM Special Class

8:00 PM English study & work

12:00 AM Sleep

Waking up early and working on the school rooftop

is really productive, plus I get morning sunlight,

so I'll keep that routine going.

In my case, classes end at 4:30 PM,

so I'll run and shower at that time, take the evening Special Class, and then do English prep and review and FOMUS work before bed.

Since coming to Cebu,

I've made early rising a daily habit, and with running on top of that, I'm living a way healthier lifestyle than when I was in Japan, which is kind of funny.

But that's how it is, and

I'll tackle week 6 starting tomorrow with this routine!

The Special Class textbook

It's packed with vocabulary useful for business

excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Those Who Take Action Win." ▼Conversation Is All About Whether You Want to Talk to That Person Week 6 classes have begun. As I wrote in the last article, the teacher swap went exactly as I'd hoped.
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about the theme "Those Who Take Action Win."

▼Conversation Is All About Whether You Want to Talk to That Person

Week 6 classes have begun.

As I wrote in the last article,

the teacher swap went exactly as I'd hoped.

On top of that,

I've added a new vocabulary class,

fully optimizing my environment for learning English.

And today was actually

the first class after the teacher swap was completed.

I went in with different expectations than before,

and...

It was even better than I expected!!!

It really paid off to swap teachers

every week and carefully select the best ones.

In my case, I need English skills I can use in real-world situations,

so I need to train my speaking and listening.

And for that, I feel it's better to do training that can only be done on-site.

Plus, I'm a working professional with only 3 months,

so what I can do is limited.

So classes that just go through textbooks

are fine for the college students,

but they're not enough for me.

*I study, but not textbook-based study

So I've told most teachers to skip the textbook

and train my English skills

through conversation.

Since I've told them about running my own business, the teachers understand and go along with it, which makes things easy!

With the teacher I first met 2 weeks ago,

I'd already exchanged Instagram at that point,

so she'd been seeing my posts

and already had a general idea of what I do.

That made it much easier to communicate

"this is how I want to use our time together."

As a result, with that teacher too,

we went with a no-textbook style.

After 5 weeks of swapping teachers every week,

I've finally managed to assemble what I personally consider

a great lineup of teachers for my classes.

Learning English has become

more fun than ever.

In the end, the best way to improve your English

is to talk with people you actually want to talk to!

▼The Model Search Continues

Now, shifting to

the FOMUS model search.

I've barely gotten any replies.

A few trickle in here and there,

but it's mostly things like "I'm not available that day."

As expected, it's been a struggle.

I have zero connections,

and on top of that, it's an offer from a foreigner,

so it's understandable if they're suspicious.

Still, I keep sending offers

to as many models as I can.

I've been telling my English teachers about this too, and

one teacher has been helping me search for models.

And guess what — that teacher's model friend

might agree to participate!!

A connection through a teacher, of all things!!

And on top of that, that model said

she'd invite her model friends too,

so suddenly the possibility of Masu Photo shoots opened up from a completely unexpected direction.

And there's more!

While I've been desperately

sending messages to models every day,

one actually replied saying she'd love to participate!!

I'm incredibly happy!

I immediately started corresponding with her,

and we're working out the schedule.

If things go well, the shoot could happen in April.

I knew 90% of them would ignore me,

but sending those passionate offers day after day

has paid off.

Whether it's swapping English teachers

or making Masu Photo model offers for FOMUS,

in the end, those who take action win!!

I was reminded once again that the key is to take more action than anyone else and keep going until results come.

I'll keep sending offers to Filipino models,

and use this experience

to refine my Masu Photo strategy for the next country!!

Went for a run after class

title_en: Keep a Near-Term Goal in Sight
excerpt_en: Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS! Today I'll be talking about the theme "Keep a Near-Term Goal in Sight." ▼Officially Signed Up for the Medoc Marathon! So, the topic suddenly shifts away from Cebu, but I've officially decided to participate in the Medoc Marathon held in Bordeaux, France on September 7, 2024.
body_en:
Hey there, it's MaSU from FOMUS!

Today I'll be talking about the theme "Keep a Near-Term Goal in Sight."

▼Officially Signed Up for the Medoc Marathon!

So, the topic suddenly shifts away

from Cebu, but

I've officially decided to participate in the "Medoc Marathon" held in Bordeaux, France on September 7, 2024.

*I wrote about it in detail in article #20 as well

https://www.notion.so/20-8268d0dd77ed43819c0d67ee44684daa?pvs=4

There are several reasons, but

the highlights of this marathon are that

you can drink wine at the rest stops,

and it's totally normal to run in costume.

And

the costume theme changes every year.

This year's theme is:

"MAKE YOUR GAMES"

Which can also be interpreted as "create your own game."

As I'm writing this article, what I'm thinking is:

since the game of expressing yourself using a masu is a great fit for FOMUS, if I can create a costume incorporating masu and run in it, I could use the masu as a conversation starter to connect with more people.

Plus, with lots of media present, there's a chance to get FOMUS known.

No matter what,

my head always goes to work stuff, but

there probably won't be another person who runs the Medoc Marathon wearing masu.

That's exactly why I think this year's "MAKE YOUR GAMES" theme is a perfect match.

So that's the news —

my participation in the Medoc Marathon

is officially confirmed as of today.

And,

here's where today's title comes in.

The Medoc Marathon is in September.

That means I have about half a year.

I'll use this time to run every day,

build up my stamina, and get my body ready to run 42 km.

Before coming to Cebu, I never ran on my own

because I didn't see a reason to,

but for health reasons, and because all my friends around me run, I wanted to join them — that was part of it too.

All sorts of factors came together, and

it ultimately led to "I'm going to run the Medoc Marathon."

Also, I simply want to be able to say

my first-ever full marathon was the Medoc Marathon.

And with only half a year to prepare,

here in Cebu I run 6 days a week,

at least 5 km each day.

*One day off per week

https://www.notion.so

Having these kinds of near-term goals

is incredibly effective when you're working toward something.

In my case, because the Medoc Marathon is coming up,

it transforms into a strong motivation to run every day!

If I didn't have the Medoc Marathon as a goal,

I probably wouldn't be doing these daily runs.

I felt today that keeping a near-term goal in sight

is really important.

▼Same Goes for English

It's exactly the same with English.

Because I decided "this year, I'm taking on Europe,"

I created an environment where I use English more for work,

and because I fully understand that I'll regret it in the field if I don't study now,

I'm carving out time — even cutting into work hours —

to create an environment for studying English.

Honestly, I've lost a huge amount of time for work and information gathering, so I'm in a position where I have to operate more efficiently.

That's tough in its own way,

but it's also an opportunity for growth.

Also, I just started the new English vocabulary class, and the other students have better English than me.

For the first time in a while, I'm in an environment where I'm the only one who can't understand the teacher's English.

It's pretty tough, but there's a part of me that's happy about it too.

It feels like jumping into a game I can't easily clear,

and I believe this is where I can really level up.

Changing your environment,

and on top of that, setting a near-term goal —

I believe that leads to even better work than before.

So today's article was mainly about the Medoc Marathon.

Exercise, English, business —

I need to train on all these fronts,

and my time management in Cebu is tight.

But the fact that I can even enjoy all of that

is partly because I get to write about it in these articles.

Once again, it's pretty cool that there's something to write about every day here in Cebu.

See you next time!
